title: Gemma Playground: Parallel Agents in Action
summary: Ian Ballantyne from Google DeepMind demonstrates Gemma 4's multi-agent capabilities, running 10 independent subagents locally with an orchestrator pattern. The system achieves inference speeds above 170 tokens per second and autonomously generates an SVG art gallery through parallel task delegation.
writeup: Ian Ballantyne demonstrates Gemma 4 26B running locally, with a single controller orchestrating 10 parallel subagents that complete an SVG art gallery in seconds. The demo covers batch processing and concurrent autonomous agent execution on one machine, with inference above 170 tokens per second. The takeaway: a multi-agent orchestration pattern can run on local hardware at meaningful throughput, without relying on a remote service.
